::: panel-tabset

### R dplyr


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(dplyr)
# subset a few columns for simplicity
sw <- starwars[, 1:7]
```
:::


### Python pandas

::: {.cell}

```{.python .cell-code}
import pandas as pd
# this imports the sw dataset from the R code chunk
sw = r.sw
```
:::

:::

## Subset rows and columns by index

::: panel-tabset

### dplyr


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# rows
sw[1:3, ]
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
# A tibble: 3 x 7
  name           height  mass hair_color skin_color  eye_color birth_year
  <chr>           <int> <dbl> <chr>      <chr>       <chr>          <dbl>
1 Luke Skywalker    172    77 blond      fair        blue              19
2 C-3PO             167    75 <NA>       gold        yellow           112
3 R2-D2              96    32 <NA>       white, blue red               33
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# columns
sw[, 1:3]
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
# A tibble: 87 x 3
   name               height  mass
   <chr>               <int> <dbl>
 1 Luke Skywalker        172    77
 2 C-3PO                 167    75
 3 R2-D2                  96    32
 4 Darth Vader           202   136
 5 Leia Organa           150    49
 6 Owen Lars             178   120
 7 Beru Whitesun lars    165    75
 8 R5-D4                  97    32
 9 Biggs Darklighter     183    84
10 Obi-Wan Kenobi        182    77
# … with 77 more rows
```
:::
:::


### pandas


::: {.cell}

```{.python .cell-code}
# rows
sw.iloc[0:3]   
# columns
```

```{.python .cell-code}
sw.iloc[:, 0:3]
```
:::


:::
